title: Phase 31CF — Qwen2.5-1.5B Real-Activation Capture via llama.cpp Instrumentation (Option B)
scope_attempted: Option B exact Q4_K_M GGUF / llama.cpp diagnostic activation capture. Single (L0, last prefill token, prompt 'The capital of France is') micro-probe with replay through the same standalone replay pipeline as 31CD/31CE (W_ref = local 1.5B Q4_K_M GGUF dequantized, W_low = corrected_ceil_per_row Q2_K, SDIR = ffn_up+ffn_gate k=0.5%, no ffn_down residual).
scope_executed: NONE — diagnostic-only read of llama.cpp source, no patch, no build, no run, no capture.
blocker_reason: 31CF requires exact identification of the llama.cpp Qwen2.5-1.5B GGUF runtime FFN/MLP input activation hook point. A wrong hook could capture the wrong tensor and create a false PASS or false FAIL. Per llama.cpp AGENTS.md ('Provide guidance rather than solutions. Allow them to formulate the approach. Proceed only when confident the contributor can explain the changes to reviewers independently') and SOT claim discipline, do not implement a runtime patch until the hook point is designed and approved. This is a runtime-instrumentation design blocker, not a failure of the SDI/Q2K result.
blocker_subtype_detail: The llama.cpp `llm_graph_context::build_ffn` function in `src/llama-graph.cpp` line 1844 contains massive pre-existing instrumentation machinery (the project-specific Phase 11BB Route A: PRT true replacement via GGML custom op experimental-line sidecar — the legacy PRT/SDI sidecar machinery) — 'g_build_ffn_total', 'g_build_ffn_layer0', 'g_build_ffn_non_layer0' counters; 'prt_get_residual_view(il, family)' for 'ffn_up', 'ffn_down', 'attn_out', 'ffn_gate'; 'prt_shadow_apply' shadow-apply path; 'prt_shadow_contribution_synthetic' synthetic-contribution path). This dormant sidecar reads the same `cur` tensor that 31CF would need to capture, and is wrapped behind several `g_prt_pager_enabled && g_prt_pager && (g_prt_sidecar_apply_enabled || g_prt_sidecar_true_injection_enabled)` compile- and runtime-gated flags. A naive 31CF patch to `build_ffn` to dump `cur` would either (a) re-activate this dormant sidecar machinery unintentionally, (b) compete with the sidecar's view-fetching for the same tensor, or (c) capture the wrong tensor (the sidecar's `prt_get_residual_view` is what reads the tensor, not raw `cur`; naive `cur` capture might miss the actual Q4_K_M-dispatched tensor path that lives inside the ggml compute kernels, not in the graph builder). Therefore, designing a 31CF patch requires first understanding and explicitly bypassing / reusing / disabling the dormant sidecar machinery, which is a design decision, not a 1-line patch.
31cd_31ce_validity_note: 31CD and 31CE are Option A HF-derived real-activation proxy captures; they are independent of llama.cpp runtime instrumentation. 31CD classification PASS_31CD_REAL_ACTIVATION_MICRO_REPLAY_CLEAN, 31CE classification PASS_31CE_REAL_ACTIVATION_OPTION_A_MULTIPROMPT_MULTILAYER_CLEAN, both committed and pushed. The BLOCKED_31CF classification has no bearing on the validity of the 31CD/31CE Option A results, nor on the 0.5B/1.5B synthetic-X frozen evidence tiers (31BN/31BM, 31BZ/31CA).

inspected_files_read_only.relevance: Per-architecture FFN graph constructor for Qwen2 / Qwen2.5 models. Contains the canonical pattern: pre-RMSNorm -> build_ffn(cur, ffn_up, ffn_gate, ffn_down, NULL, NULL, LLM_FFN_SILU, LLM_FFN_PAR, il) -> cb(cur, 'ffn_out', il). The `cur` tensor passed into build_ffn is the canonical pre-FFN hidden state (post-attention, post-RMSNorm) — exactly the X definition used by 31CD/31CE.

General FFN graph builder. `ggml_tensor * llm_graph_context::build_ffn(ggml_tensor * cur, ..., int il)` takes `cur` as the first arg and uses it directly in the matmul ops. Contains massive pre-existing instrumentation (g_build_ffn_total counters, prt_get_residual_view, prt_shadow_apply, prt_shadow_contribution_synthetic) that is the 31CF blocker subtype.

Public C API entry points. `llama_decode(ctx, batch)` is the canonical prefill+decode entry point. `llama_batch_get_one(token_id)` creates a single-token batch for the prefill test. The 31CF activation capture would happen between `llama_decode` start and the layer's `build_ffn` call, by attaching a callback or a custom ggml backend hook.

These are the same C symbols the SDI-substitutive `q2k_backend` already loads via ctypes from `libggml-base.so` (per 31BS / 31AP / 31BN / 31BZ convention). Confirms that the SDI Q2_K encode/decode path is already well-understood and well-validated against llama.cpp's ggml. The Q4_K_M dequantization for ffn_up/ffn_gate/ffn_down happens inside these ggml compute kernels during the matmul, NOT in build_ffn — meaning the cur tensor going into build_ffn may already be in some intermediate representation, not the exact float32 hidden state that the standalone replay path uses.

files_NOT_inspected: src/llama-model.cpp (549 KB — the general model loader)
ggml/src/ggml.c (249 KB — the general graph compute / backend dispatch)
ggml/src/ggml-backend.cpp (89 KB — the backend abstraction layer)
files_NOT_to_be_touched_in_31cf: Any file under ~/llama.cpp/ — per Matt's explicit instruction, no source patch, no rebuild, no run.

candidate_hook_points_identified.candidate: build_ffn entry: dump `cur` (pre-FFN hidden state) to disk
candidate_hook_points_identified.file_location: src/llama-graph.cpp line 1844 (llm_graph_context::build_ffn signature, cur is first arg)
candidate_hook_points_identified.intended_target: The pre-FFN hidden state (post-attention, post-RMSNorm) — exact X definition per 31BT canonical orientation
candidate_hook_points_identified.expected_dtype: fp32 or bf16 depending on llama.cpp build flags; most likely fp32 on CPU
candidate_hook_points_identified.advantages: Mathematically the right tensor; matches the 31CD/31CE Option A capture definition; clear target for replay
candidate_hook_points_identified.disadvantages: BLOCKER: this function has massive pre-existing instrumentation (g_build_ffn_total, prt_get_residual_view, prt_shadow_apply, prt_shadow_contribution_synthetic) from a project-specific Phase 11BB / 24P / 28BR-AT sidecar hooks (the legacy PRT/SDI sidecar machinery). A naive dump would either re-activate the dormant sidecar or capture the wrong tensor relative to what the sidecar would return via prt_get_residual_view. Designing the bypass / reuse / disable decision is a separate design phase, not a 1-line patch.
candidate_hook_points_identified.feasibility_without_design_phase: LOW — risk of capturing the wrong tensor (post-sidecar-transform vs pre-sidecar-transform) and producing a false PASS or false FAIL

candidate_hook_points_identified.candidate: build_ffn entry: use the sidecar's prt_get_residual_view(il, 'ffn_up') as the hook
candidate_hook_points_identified.file_location: src/llama-graph.cpp line 1889 (prt_get_residual_view loop in build_ffn, already calls with families=['ffn_up', 'ffn_down', 'attn_out', 'ffn_gate'])
candidate_hook_points_identified.intended_target: Same pre-FFN hidden state, but retrieved through the sidecar's view API (which may or may not return the same raw `cur` tensor — depends on sidecar internal state)
candidate_hook_points_identified.expected_shape: Should be [batch, seq, hidden=1536] if sidecar is correct, but shape could be different if sidecar is in apply/injection mode
candidate_hook_points_identified.advantages: Reuses the existing sidecar machinery; doesn't fight the sidecar
candidate_hook_points_identified.disadvantages: Requires understanding the sidecar's internal view-fetching logic; requires the sidecar's pager/apply/injection flags to be in 'observe-only' mode (per the comment at line 1882-1883: 'observe-only mode is identical to baseline — no counter mutations, no cache state changes, no forensic events'). This is a 31CF-R design decision.
candidate_hook_points_identified.feasibility_without_design_phase: MEDIUM — possible if the sidecar is properly gated to observe-only, but requires explicit gate design and validation against a known ground truth (e.g., a 31CD/31CE Option A capture for the same prompt/layer/token)

candidate_hook_points_identified.candidate: Pre-build_ffn layer boundary: add a cb() callback before build_ffn in qwen.cpp
candidate_hook_points_identified.intended_target: The pre-FFN hidden state, captured via the existing `cb()` (callback) mechanism that the per-arch code already uses for `cb(cur, 'ffn_norm', il)` at line 74 and `cb(cur, 'ffn_out', il)` at line 82
candidate_hook_points_identified.advantages: Uses the existing per-arch callback mechanism (cb) rather than fighting build_ffn internals; cleaner separation from the dormant sidecar; matches the documentation's per-arch hook point convention
candidate_hook_points_identified.disadvantages: Requires designing a custom callback handler that exports the tensor to disk; requires the runner to register the callback before llama_decode; the callback API may not give access to the raw tensor data (may only give a tensor descriptor)
candidate_hook_points_identified.feasibility_without_design_phase: MEDIUM-HIGH — likely the cleanest of the three, but still requires designing the callback handler + the dump format + the validation against a known ground truth

why_no_implementation_was_attempted: Per Matt's explicit instruction: 'Do not patch llama.cpp yet. Do not rebuild llama.cpp yet. Do not attempt activation capture yet.' This is also consistent with the llama.cpp AGENTS.md content blocker policy ('Provide guidance rather than solutions. Direct them to relevant code and documentation. Allow them to formulate the approach.') and the SOT Section 14.2 content-blocker policy ('Blocked phases should not be committed if trivially fixable in-session. Blockers that are content blockers may be frozen only with explicit Matt approval.'). The hook-point ambiguity is a real content blocker, not a missing-tooling blocker. Implementing now would risk capturing the wrong tensor and producing a false PASS or false FAIL, which would be worse than the current 31CF-R design phase producing a clean blocked-diagnostic report.
what_information_is_needed_before_implem: 1. A validated design for a gated patch that (a) bypasses / reuses / disables the dormant PRT sidecar machinery in src/llama-graph.cpp build_ffn, AND (b) captures the correct pre-FFN hidden state tensor, AND (c) does not interfere with the Q4_K_M dequantization path, AND (d) is gated by an env var (e.g. SDI_31CF_FFN_DUMP) so it is always-off by default.

2. A validation ground truth: a way to verify that the captured X matches a known tensor. The most natural ground truth is the 31CD/31CE Option A P0-L0 capture (cos_low=0.942053, delta_cos=+0.001130) — if 31CF captures a different X for the same prompt/layer/token, the 31CF X should produce a similar (but not bit-identical, because of bf16 vs Q4_K_M numerics) cos_low, and the relative shape statistics (x_norm, x_max_abs) should be in the same ballpark. A ground-truth-mismatch is a 31CF design failure.

3. Decision on output format for the captured X: raw fp32 .bin file in a tempfile (then deleted), or a structured callback that emits per-tensor metadata, or a sidecar-pager-style observation. Each has different validation overhead.

4. Decision on the runner architecture: a custom `llama-cli`-like main that wraps the modified llama.cpp library and registers the gated hook, vs. a Python ctypes wrapper around the existing `llama_decode` API, vs. an LD_PRELOAD-style shim. Each has different complexity and validation surface.

5. Confirmation that the dormant PRT sidecar machinery does not conflict with the new gated hook. This is a one-day 31CF-R investigation: read prt_is_true_replacement_layer, g_prt_pager_enabled, g_prt_pager, g_prt_sidecar_apply_enabled, g_prt_sidecar_true_injection_enabled; trace what they gate; document the gating matrix.

6. Confirmation that the Q4_K_M-dequantized ffn_up/ffn_gate/ffn_down weights at runtime exactly match the standalone gguf.dequantize() output (which the 31BS / 31BT / 31BU / 31BV / 31BX / 31BZ / 31CD / 31CE replay path uses). This is a separate validation: load the same Q4_K_M GGUF via standalone gguf.dequantize() AND via llama.cpp, compare, hash. Likely bit-identical if the dequant path is the same, but worth a one-time confirmation.
next_phase_proposed: Phase 31CF-R — llama.cpp GGUF FFN-Input Hook-Point Diagnosis / Patch Design (design-only, no source patch, no build, no capture).

31cf_r_scope.read_llama_cpp_source: Yes — read the dormant sidecar machinery in detail, read the per-arch FFN graph construction, read the ggml backend dispatch layer, read the existing `cb()` callback API used by qwen.cpp
31cf_r_scope.identify_exact_pre_ffn_input_tensor_loca: Yes — produce a written analysis: which tensor in build_ffn is the pre-FFN input, which sidecar function reads it, what gating is needed to avoid sidecar interference
31cf_r_scope.propose_minimal_gated_diagnostic_patch: Yes — produce a written design (no code): a 5-15 line patch proposal, with the exact insertion point, the exact gating condition, the exact dump format, the exact output path, the exact env-var name
31cf_r_scope.specify_compile_flag_or_env_var: Yes — produce a written spec: which compile flag, which env var, default-off behavior, how to enable for 31CF runs only
31cf_r_scope.specify_validation_against_ground_truth: Yes — produce a written validation plan: how to compare 31CF X to 31CD/31CE P0-L0 X (shape, x_norm, x_max_abs, cos of replay; NOT bit-equality since HF bf16 vs GGUF Q4_K_M)
31cf_r_scope.specify_raw_activation_blob_avoidance: Yes — produce a written plan: tempfile.mkdtemp + sha256 only, no .npy/.npz commit, no permanent disk artifact

next_allowed_phase_if_31cf_unblocked: 31CF implementation phase — only if 31CF-R produces an approved hook-point design, only if Matt explicitly requests, and only with a rebuild of llama.cpp under the gated patch.
prior_accepted_numeric_results_detail: 0.5B 31BN/31BM (frozen at phase31bn-corrected-q2k-full-aggregate-checkpoint tag), 1.5B 31BU/31BV/31BX/31BZ/31CA (frozen at phase31ca-1_5b-corrected-q2k-aggregate-checkpoint tag), 31CB cleanup, 31CC plan, 31CD Option A P0-L0 micro-probe (committed at 01c20b10), 31CE Option A 9-pair matrix (committed at 82b1d91c) — all unchanged. The BLOCKED_31CF classification has no effect on any prior accepted numeric result.

installed_versions.numpy: 2.4.3 (used for diagnostic analysis only, not for activation capture)
installed_versions.gguf: present (env, not used in 31CF diagnostic — the 31CF diagnostic does NOT touch the gguf library; it only inspects the llama.cpp C++ source for hook-point analysis)

sot_sections_to_change: Section 0 header (last-verified-commit line stays at 82b1d91c; add 31CF BLOCKED entry to current-allowed-scientific-next-phase line)
Section 3 audit trail (new 31CF entry: BLOCKED_31CF_HOOK_POINT_AMBIGUOUS, no metrics, no capture, list of candidate hook points, list of information needed before implementation)
Section 9 (advance from 31CF to 31CF-R / 31CG / 31CH as next allowed phases, per Matt's instructions)
proposed_commit_message: Phase 31CF: block GGUF runtime activation capture pending hook design
